title: Cycling, Croissants and Cape Adventures
description: GEORGE NEWS - While between covering two...
bodyText:
Video

GEORGE NEWS - While between covering two stories, Marguerite van Ginkel, journalist from the George Herald, stopped at Rocket Café in George for a quick almond croissant and take-away coffee. That’s when she noticed a group of very tall men in cycling gear, speaking with unmistakable Swiss accents, followed by a photographer wielding a serious-looking camera. Naturally, her curiosity and a touch of amusement got the better of her, and she just had to ask what was going on.


> It turns out this is close-knit group of friends from Switzerland, led by former politician and cycling enthusiast Ruedi Noser, recently kicked off an extraordinary two-week cycling journey through the Western Cape.


The tour celebrates friendship, endurance and the joy of exploring South Africa's open roads.

Swiss cycling group exploring Route 62. Photo: Fraser Schenck

This 15-night adventure along the iconic Route 62 is more than a cycling challenge, it is a shared experience of discovery. From the lush coast of George to the vineyards of Stellenbosch, the group is covering over 900km and 6 000m of climbing, supported by expert guides, mechanics and a dedicated on-road crew.


> VIDEO: Watch the video provided by video Current Media:


Your browser does not support HTML5 video.

Behind the seamless organisation are two passionate travel specialists: Tri-Travel SA, known for fully supported cycling experiences, and RacePace Tours, specialising in bespoke, sport-specific tours world-wide. Together they have crafted a journey that balances mountain passes and open roads with fine food, rest and authentic local hospitality.

The cycling group of friends from Switzerland en route. Photo: Fraser Schenck

Noser added that this tour is the best boost for body and mind - the perfect fusion of sport, travel and friendship. Noser, a serial entrepreneur, business advocate and former member of the Swiss Parliament, says cycling in South Africa's summer almost feels like riding in Switzerland's, just a bit warmer.

The adventure will conclude with a farewell lunch at the Ernie Els Wine Estate in Stellenbosch next week, marking the end of an unforgettable ride and the beginning of lasting memories on Route 62.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
